title: Rousing Nutty Jamaican King Prawns Bowl
description: A vibrant, nutty bowl of seasoned king prawns with coconut quinoa, lime, and fresh herbs, chilled in an ice-bath for a crunchy, energizing Jamaican treat.
instructions: Rinse the diced red bell pepper and finely chopped red onion in cold water and set aside.
In a saucepan, combine the coconut quinoa with 300ml of water, bring to a boil, then simmer until fluffy, about 15 minutes.
While the quinoa cooks, heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and add the peeled king prawns.
Sprinkle the Jamaican jerk seasoning and a pinch of salt over the prawns, then stir-fry until pink and opaque, about 3 minutes per side.
Once cooked, transfer the prawns to a bowl and let them rest for a minute.
In a large mason jar, layer the cooked quinoa, stir in the diced bell pepper, chopped onion, and cilantro.
Add the seared prawns on top of the quinoa mixture, then drizzle with the fresh lime juice and sprinkle the lime zest.
Gently fold in the roasted cashews, ensuring an even distribution of nutty crunch.
Fill the jar with ice cubes to create an ice-bath, then seal the lid and refrigerate for at least 10 minutes for a shock chill.
Before serving, give the bowl a quick stir, garnish with extra cilantro if desired, and enjoy the energizing, low-glycemic Jamaican flavor.
